input.prompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

data.content:
Use the `IsTestProject` property, which is automatically set to `true` by the `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` package that your test projects already reference:

```xml
<!-- Directory.Build.props -->
<PropertyGroup Condition="'$(IsTestProject)' == 'true'">
  <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
  <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
  <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
</PropertyGroup>
```

This works because `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` sets `IsTestProject=true` during evaluation. Your library projects never reference that package, so the condition is `false` and they stay as libraries.

**Alternative** – if you prefer an explicit convention (e.g., all test projects end in `.Tests`):

```xml
<PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
  ...
</PropertyGroup>
```

The `IsTestProject` approach is more robust since it's driven by the actual SDK reference rather than a naming convention.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about how to conditionally set properties in a `Directory.Build.props` file so that `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP) runner properties only apply to test projects, not library/production projects.

This is a common pattern when migrating from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform. The key is to use an MSBuild condition that detects whether a project is a test project.

The standard way to do this is to check for the `IsTestProject` property, which is set to `true` by the `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` package (or you can set it yourself). Another approach is to use a condition based on project naming conventions or checking for a package reference.


I'm wrapping the OutputType and MTP runner properties in a PropertyGroup with a condition that checks if `IsTestProject` equals true. Since the `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` package automatically sets this property on test projects, this is the cleanest and most reliable approach.
